title: UnderControl Note File
description: Schema for UnderControl note files — YAML frontmatter + markdown body. Notes are attached to tasks via task_id. This is the single source of truth for all clients.

id.description: Note UUID. Present = existing note (update). Absent = new note (create).

task_id.description: UUID of the parent task this note belongs to. Required. Aligns with CLI 'ud task note apply' format.

created_at.description: Creation timestamp (ISO 8601). Set by server on creation. Clients should not modify.

updated_at.description: Last update timestamp (ISO 8601). Updated by server.

body.description: Markdown content after the frontmatter closing '---'. Becomes the note content.

file_format.description:
The complete file is a markdown file with YAML frontmatter:

---
id: {uuid}
task_id: {parent-task-uuid}
created_at: 2025-01-01T00:00:00Z
updated_at: 2025-01-01T00:00:00Z
---

Note content in markdown...

filename_convention.description:
Note files are named: {normalizeFilename(taskTitle)}-{normalizeFilename(noteFirstLine)}.md

noteFirstLine: first non-empty line of the note body content, processed through normalizeFilename().

The taskTitle prefix associates the note with its parent task visually in the filesystem.

Duplicate handling: append '-2', '-3' suffix only when collision detected.

Same normalizeFilename rules as task files (see task.v1.schema.json).

create_vs_update.description:
Declarative semantics (kubectl-style):
- No 'id' in frontmatter → CREATE new note under the specified task_id
- 'id' present → UPDATE existing note

This applies to both CLI 'ud task note apply' and local sync file scanning.
